nameMeanings.name: Gordon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
Jewish (from Russia): variant of   Gordon   5.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: First
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Fürst (see Furst ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): artificial name from Yiddish first ‘prince’.
Slovenian and Croatian (Firšt): nickname from firšt ‘prince’, an archaic or dialect loanword from German Fürst (see Furst and above).

variants.name: Furst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eugen, Kurt, Gunter, Hans, Herta, Maximilian, Ulrich.

German and Swiss German (Fürst): nickname from Old High German furisto ‘the first, most noble one’, a cognate of Old English fyrest ‘first, foremost’. The surname Fürst is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia. Compare Feerst , Fierce , Fierst , First , and Fuerst .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Fürst): artificial name from German Fürst ‘prince’. Compare Fuerst .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gordon First of Lochinvar was born on 26 February 1420, in Stitchill, Roxburghshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Roger Gordon 4th Of Stitchill, was 20 and his mother, Annabella Drummond, was 15. He had at least 4 sons with Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton. He died in 1470, at the age of 50, and was buried in Dumfries, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland, United Kingdom.

Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton. Il est décédé en 1470, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Dumfries, Dumfries and Galloway, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Gordon First of Lochinvarが1420年2月26日にイギリススコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアスティトチルで生まれた時，父Roger Gordon 4th Of Stitchillは20歳，母Annabella Drummondは15歳であった。彼はPrincess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Mortonとの間に少なくとも4人の息子をもうけた。彼は1470年に50歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドダムフリーズ アンド ガロワイダムフリーズにて埋葬された。

ko.Dumfries, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotlan: 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리스 앤드 갈로아이 드프리스
ko.lifeSummary: William Gordon First of Lochinvar(이)가 1420년 2월 26일 영국 스코틀랜드 록스버그h실르 스티트치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Gordon 4th Of Stitchill(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Annabella Drummond(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1470년 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리스 앤드 갈로아이 드프리스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Gordon First of Lochinvar родился 26 февраля 1420 г. в местности стичилл, роксбургшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger Gordon 4th Of Stitchill было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Annabella Drummond, 15. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Princess Joan Gordon of Stitchill born Stewart, Princess of Scotland, Countess of Morton. Он умер в 1470 г. в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности думфриесь, думфриесь анд галлоуеи, Шотландия, Великобритания.
